content:
# Viscoelasticity

**Summary**: The property of materials that exhibit both viscous and elastic characteristics.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018]] used viscoelastic measurements to characterize the drilling fluid structure.

- [[2023-alameer|Alameer, 2023]] investigated the effect of polymer viscoelasticity (N2 values 1.173, 1.6554, 1.925) on injectivity loss.

- [[2023-dafaalla|Dafaalla, 2023]] investigated the role of viscoelasticity in reducing residual oil saturation (SOR) at high flow rates.

## Scientific Context

- [[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018]] Viscoelasticity is critical for maintaining suspension in low shear rates.

- [[2023-alameer|Alameer, 2023]] investigated the effect of polymer viscoelasticity (N2 values 1.173, 1.6554, 1.925) on injectivity loss.

- [[2023-dafaalla|Dafaalla, 2023]] investigated the role of viscoelasticity in reducing residual oil saturation (SOR) at high flow rates.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018]] Analyzed via oscillatory tests.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Storage modulus (G') increased by 40% with copolymer addition. ([[2018-basfar|BASFAR, 2018, p. 66]])


- The impact of viscoelasticity becomes significant only when the polymer viscosity is high; at low viscosities, it has a negligible effect on well length requirements. ([[2023-alameer|Alameer, 2023, p. 76]])

- Viscoelastic effects are particularly significant for improving oil recovery in heterogeneous reservoirs during shear-thickening regimes. ([[2023-dafaalla|Dafaalla, 2023, p. 41]])
